meta.title: Convert MOV to MP4 — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Turn QuickTime .mov files into universal .mp4 right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your video never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MOV to MP4 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Change .mov to .mp4 locally in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.
hero.title: Convert MOV to MP4 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop a QuickTime .mov file and get a universal .mp4 that plays everywhere — phones, Discord, email, editors. It's converted on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.p1: .mov is Apple's QuickTime format — great on Macs and iPhones, but plenty of apps, websites and Windows tools would rather have .mp4. Converting to MP4 (H.264) gives you a file that plays virtually anywhere, with no codec surprises.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your clip to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the conversion runs locally using your browser's built-in video engine, so even large recordings convert without a slow upload, and nothing is stored or logged.
faq.a1: No. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.a2: Converting re-encodes the video, so there's some loss, but at the default settings it's hard to spot. You can also pick a target size or a quality level before converting.
faq.q3: Does it work for long iPhone recordings?
faq.a3: Yes. On Chrome or Edge, files up to about 2 GB are supported. Very large files on other browsers may be declined with a clear message rather than failing silently.
